scope: SEGSCAN stability probe -- hour-by-hour finiteness of the coupled forecast advanced by the host-loop segment
method: each hour advanced by the SAME compiled 180-step _advance_chunk segment (proven bitwise-equal to the single scan in segscan_equiv.json); finite ranges read at each hour boundary
termination: EXTERNAL SIGTERM (exit 143) at lead ~8h -- the probe process was killed by the environment, NOT by a model NaN; the walk had been emitting finite, stable hourly records (theta 289-495 K, w_max ~1.3 m/s) right up to the kill. The later hours (9-24) were not reached by this probe.

separate_observation_24h_final_state.source: segscan_24h.json (full 24h run, guards OFF, same config)
separate_observation_24h_final_state.note: The full 24h guards-OFF run RAN TO COMPLETION (bounded compile + bounded 10.2 GB peak) but its FINAL state was all-NaN, so the coupled model loses finiteness somewhere in leads 9-24h under guards OFF. This is a MODEL stability limit, NOT a segmentation defect: the segment is bitwise-equal to the single scan, so the single fused scan would produce the identical NaN. The operational skill is only validated to +3h; idealized cases are the honest dycore gate.

conclusion: Segmentation is feasible + equivalent + memory-bounded to 24h. The coupled MODEL is finite/stable for at least the first 8h guards-OFF (covering the validated +3h skill window) but loses finiteness later guards-OFF -- a pre-existing model-stability question separate from the segmentation mechanism.
